title: Human leucine zipper/trail recombinant polypeptides
abstract: Disclosed are recombinant polypeptides that include human TRAIL sequences as well as human leucine zipper motifs as well as polynucleotides that encode the recombinant polypeptides, expression vectors that comprise the polynucleotides, pharmaceutical compositions comprising the polypeptides, and methods of using the pharmaceutical compositions for the treatment of cancer. Further disclosed are TRAIL sequences with stabilizing mutations and methods of expressing the disclosed polypeptides in E. coli.
claims: Claims 1. A recombinant polypeptide comprising: a first sequence that is at least 90% identical to SEQ ID NO: 21 and a second sequence comprising a human leucine zipper motif with 'a' and 'd' isoleucine substitutions. 2. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 1 where the second sequence comprises SEQ ID NO: 1-SEQ ID NO: 19. 3. The recombinant polypeptide of claims 1-2 further comprising a linker between the first sequence and the second sequence. 4. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 3 where the linker has a sequence of KGSG, GSG, SG, or RGSG. 5. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 4 comprising SEQ ID NO: 20. 6. The recombinant polypeptide of claims 1-2 where the first sequence comprises one or more stabilizing mutations. 7. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 6 comprising an S133P, S156C, L196C, T127C, or H270C mutation 8. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 7 comprising SEQ ID NO: 22-SEQ ID NO: 28. 9. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 8 where the polypeptide lacks a linker between the first sequence and the second sequence. 10. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 9 comprising SEQ ID NO: 29-SEQ ID NO: 35 11. A polynucleotide that encodes the recombinant polypeptide of claims 1-10. 12. The polynucleotide of claim 11 where the polynucleotide is provided in an expression vector and where the expression vector comprises a promoter operably linked to the polynucleotide. 13. The polynucleotide of claim 11 where the polynucleotide is codon optimized for expression in yeast or E. coli. 14. A pharmaceutical composition comprising an effective amount of the recombinant polypeptide of claims 1-11 or the polynucleotide of claims 11-13 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 15. A method of treating cancer in a subject, the method comprising administering the pharmaceutical composition of claim 14 to the subject. 16. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 14 for use in treating cancer. 17. A method of expressing the polypeptide of claim 1 in an E. coli culture, the method comprising, transfecting an E. coli cell with the expression vector of claim 12, the expression vector comprising an inducible promoter, thereby producing a transfected E. coli cell; inoculating a media with the transfected E. coli cell, the media comprising zinc chloride, thereby producing the E. coli culture; inducing expression of the polypeptide of claim 1 using the inducible promoter. 18. The method of claim 17 where the media comprises at least 50 Î¼Î zinc chloride. 19. The method of claim 17-18 where the E. coli is of the strain BL21 or Rosetta. 20. The method of claim 17-18 where the E. coli is of the strain Origamiâ¢, the media further comprising 0.4M sucrose.
